params.name: Peace of Prague
params.description:
On 23 August 1866, Austria AD, which suffered a devastating defeat in the war, signed the Peace of Prague with Prussia.
Prussia sent troops to Holstein, Austria, to unify Germany, which triggered the seven-week-long war. The Prussian army won a major victory, and Prime Minister Bismarck's decision led to a lenient peace agreement with Austria. This agreement allowed Prussia to gain a lot of land while also maintaining good relations with Austria. It provided a favorable environment for Prussi …
